faqs.question: GAIA가 비공개 GitHub 리포지토리와 함께 작동하나요?
faqs.answer: 네, GAIA는 승인된 GitHub 자격 증명을 사용하며 조직 내에서 액세스 권한이 있는 비공개 리포지토리에 액세스할 수 있습니다.

faqs.question: 비 엔지니어 개발자가 GAIA에서 생성한 Notion 문서를 사용할 수 있나요?
faqs.answer: 전적으로 가능합니다. 제품 관리자, 디자이너, 리더십은 GitHub 액세스 없이 Notion에서 GitHub 인텔리전스를 얻습니다. 이것이 주요 사용 사례 중 하나입니다. 엔지니어링 활동을 전체 팀에 보이게 합니다.

google-calendar-asana.tagline: 캘린더 이벤트를 Asana 작업으로 만들고 프로젝트 작업 시간을 예약
google-calendar-asana.metaTitle: Google Calendar + Asana 자동화 - 일정 및 작업 정렬 | GAIA
google-calendar-asana.metaDescription: Automate Google Calendar and Asana with GAIA. Create Asana tasks from calendar events, block calendar time for project work, and keep your schedule and project management synchronized.
google-calendar-asana.intro: Google Calendar는 시간을 어떻게 할당하는지 알려주고 Asana는 어떤 작업을 해야 하는지 알려줍니다. 하지만 대부분의 팀은 이 둘을 별도로 관리합니다. 미팅 예약 시 해당 Asana 준비 작업이 없거나, Asana 마감일이 이를 충족할 캘린더 시간이 예약되지 않은 채 존재하며, 일정 및 작업 관리가 서로에게 정보를 제공하지 않기 때문에 프로젝트 작업이 저해됩니다. GAIA는 Google Calendar와 Asana를 연결하여 두 도구가 동일한 이야기를 하도록 합니다.

useCases.title: 캘린더 이벤트에서 미팅 준비 작업 생성
useCases.description: For every calendar event with external attendees, GAIA creates an Asana task to prepare for the meeting — review agenda, prepare talking points — assigned to the meeting host and due the day before.

useCases.title: 미팅 후 Asana의 실행 항목
useCases.description: After calendar events end, GAIA creates a follow-up Asana task to capture and distribute meeting notes and action items, ensuring meetings produce tracked outcomes.

useCases.title: 캘린더 블록 Asana 프로젝트 마일스톤
useCases.description: When an Asana project milestone has a due date, GAIA creates a Google Calendar reminder event so the deadline surfaces in your schedule with appropriate advance notice.

useCases.title: 마감일 근접 알림
useCases.description: GAIA monitors Asana tasks and detects when deadlines are approaching on days that are heavily scheduled in Google Calendar. It alerts you to reschedule meetings or extend deadlines proactively.

useCases.title: 반복 미팅을 반복 Asana 작업으로
useCases.description: Recurring calendar events like weekly standups or client check-ins generate recurring Asana tasks for prep and follow-up so standard meeting hygiene is always tracked.

howItWorks.step: Google Calendar 및 Asana를 GAIA에 연결
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Google Calendar and Asana account. Configure which Asana projects receive tasks from which calendar event types.

howItWorks.step: Define task creation and time-blocking rules
howItWorks.description: Specify which meeting types generate prep tasks, how far in advance to create them, and which Asana task due dates should create calendar reminders or time blocks.

howItWorks.step: GAIA aligns your schedule and task list
howItWorks.description: Calendar events and Asana tasks stay in sync automatically. You plan and schedule in either tool and GAIA keeps both informed.

faqs.question: Can GAIA create Asana tasks for all calendar events or only specific types?
faqs.answer: You control which event types generate Asana tasks. Common configurations include only events with external attendees, events tagged with specific calendars, or events lasting over 30 minutes.

faqs.question: Does GAIA update Asana tasks if a calendar event is rescheduled?
faqs.answer: Yes. When a calendar event changes date or time, GAIA updates the due date on the corresponding Asana prep task to match the new schedule.

faqs.question: Can GAIA help identify when Asana deadlines conflict with a busy calendar?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can audit your upcoming Asana deadlines against your Google Calendar and flag days where task deadlines and heavy meeting schedules conflict so you can plan accordingly.

google-calendar-linear.tagline: Linear 스프린트 일정을 Google Calendar와 자동으로 동기화
google-calendar-linear.metaTitle: Google Calendar + Linear 자동화 - 스프린트 일정을 캘린더로 | GAIA
google-calendar-linear.metaDescription: GAIA로 Google Calendar 및 Linear를 자동화합니다. Linear 스프린트 날짜를 Google Calendar로 동기화하고, Linear 이슈에서 미팅 컨텍스트를 얻으며, 엔지니어링 일정과 프로젝트 보드를 동기화합니다.
google-calendar-linear.intro: 엔지니어링 팀은 Linear에서 계획하지만 Google Calendar에서 일정을 잡습니다. Linear의 스프린트 시작 및 종료 날짜는 캘린더에 나타나지 않습니다. 중요한 Linear 마감일 및 주기 검토는 엔지니어가 일정을 확인하는 도구에 보이지 않습니다. GAIA는 엔지니어링 타임라인이 어디를 보든 반영되도록 두 가지를 연결합니다. Linear 주기 날짜는 Google Calendar 이벤트로 나타나고, 스프린트 계획 및 회고 캘린더 이벤트는 Linear 주기 항목을 생성합니다. 엔지니어링 일정과 프로젝트 보드는 수동 동기화 없이 동일한 이야기를 합니다.

useCases.title: Linear 주기 날짜를 Google Calendar로 동기화
useCases.description: Linear 스프린트 주기는 시작 및 종료 날짜가 있는 Google Calendar 이벤트로 나타나므로, 엔지니어와 엔지니어링 관리자는 다른 약속과 함께 스프린트 타임라인 가시성을 확보합니다.

useCases.title: 캘린더 알림으로 Linear 이슈 마감일
useCases.description: 마감일이 있는 우선순위 Linear 이슈는 Google Calendar 알림을 생성하므로, 엔지니어는 Linear에서만 보는 것이 아니라 캘린더에서 다가오는 마감일을 봅니다.

useCases.title: Linear 계획에서 미팅 준비
useCases.description: 스프린트 계획 캘린더 이벤트 전에 GAIA는 스프린트에 제안된 백로그 항목을 컴파일하고 준비 요약을 보내므로, 팀은 전체 컨텍스트를 가지고 참여합니다.

useCases.title: 검토 회의 전 회고 데이터
useCases.description: 회고 캘린더 이벤트 전에 GAIA는 Linear 주기 메트릭(완료된 이슈, 속도, 주기 시간)을 끌어와 회고가 데이터 기반이 되도록 미팅 준비 문서를 생성합니다.

useCases.title: 캘린더 이벤트로 엔지니어링 마일스톤
useCases.description: Linear 프로젝트 마일스톤은 Google Calendar 이벤트로 생성되므로, 제품 출시, 기능 동결 및 주요 결과물이 공유 팀 캘린더에 표시됩니다.

howItWorks.step: Google Calendar 및 Linear를 GAIA에 연결
howItWorks.description: Google Calendar 및 Linear 작업 공간에 GAIA를 인증합니다. 동기화할 Linear 팀 및 주기를 지정합니다.

howItWorks.step: 동기화 및 알림 규칙 구성
howItWorks.description: 어떤 Linear 이벤트가 캘린더 이벤트를 생성하는지, 어떤 이슈 마감일이 알림을 생성하는지, 어떤 캘린더 이벤트가 준비를 위해 Linear 컨텍스트를 가져와야 하는지 선택합니다.

howItWorks.step: 엔지니어링 일정 및 보드 정렬
howItWorks.description: Linear 타임라인은 자동으로 캘린더에 나타나고 캘린더 이벤트에는 Linear 컨텍스트가 첨부됩니다. 두 도구 모두 동일한 엔지니어링 일정을 반영합니다.

faqs.question: GAIA는 정의한 캘린더 날짜 범위에서 Linear 주기를 생성할 수 있나요?
faqs.answer: 네, GAIA에게 스프린트 기간을 설정한 캘린더 날짜를 기반으로 새 Linear 주기를 생성하도록 요청할 수 있으며, 올바른 시작 및 종료 날짜로 주기를 구성합니다.

faqs.question: GAIA는 모든 팀에 대한 Linear 주기를 동기화하나요, 아니면 특정 팀만 동기화하나요?
faqs.answer: 동기화할 Linear 팀을 캘린더에 구성합니다. 이는 엔지니어링 팀 캘린더를 제품 또는 디자인 팀 주기와 분리하는 데 유용합니다.

faqs.question: GAIA는 스프린트 이벤트에 대한 캘린더 초대를 팀에 보낼 수 있나요?
faqs.answer: 네, GAIA는 Linear 주기에서 Google Calendar 이벤트를 생성할 때 팀원을 게스트로 추가하도록 구성할 수 있으므로, 모든 사람의 캘린더에 스프린트 이벤트가 나타납니다.

google-calendar-zoom.tagline: Zoom 미팅 링크를 캘린더 이벤트에 자동으로 추가하고 미팅을 쉽게 관리
google-calendar-zoom.metaTitle: Google Calendar + Zoom 자동화 - 비디오 미팅 관리 | GAIA
google-calendar-zoom.metaDescription: GAIA로 Google Calendar 및 Zoom을 자동화합니다. 캘린더 이벤트에 Zoom 링크를 자동으로 생성하고, 미팅 전에 준비를 받고, 수동 조정 없이 비디오 미팅 일정을 관리합니다.
google-calendar-zoom.intro: Zoom 미팅을 생성하고 Google Calendar 이벤트에 추가하는 것은 작지만 지속적인 마찰 지점입니다. 캘린더 이벤트를 생성하고, Zoom 링크를 생성하고, 붙여넣고, 모두가 찾을 것이라고 가정합니다. 미팅이 재조정되면 Zoom 링크가 변경될 수 있습니다. 새 참석자가 추가되면 링크를 놓칠 수 있습니다. GAIA는 Google Calendar-Zoom 연결을 자동화합니다. 새 캘린더 이벤트에 Zoom 링크가 자동으로 추가됩니다. 미팅 준비는 통화 전에 제공됩니다. 미팅 후 노트는 진행된 사항을 캡처합니다. 비디오 미팅 관리의 관리 오버헤드가 사라집니다.

useCases.title: 캘린더 이벤트에 Zoom 링크 자동 추가
useCases.description: 외부 참석자와 함께 Google Calendar 이벤트를 생성하면 GAIA는 Zoom 미팅을 자동으로 생성하고 접속 URL, 미팅 ID, 비밀번호를 이벤트 설명에 추가하여 참석자가 항상 화상 통화 세부 정보를 갖도록 합니다.

useCases.title: 컨텍스트가 포함된 미팅 알림
useCases.description: 각 Zoom 연결 캘린더 이벤트 10분 전에 GAIA는 접속 링크, 참석자 목록, 공유된 아젠다, 참석자와의 최근 상호 작용 요약을 포함한 알림을 보내므로 준비되고 제시간에 도착합니다.

useCases.title: 캘린더로 Zoom 미팅 재조정
useCases.description: 캘린더 이벤트가 재조정되면 GAIA는 해당 Zoom 미팅 시간을 자동으로 업데이트하여 참석자가 항상 현재 타이밍으로 작업 가능한 접속 링크를 갖도록 합니다.

useCases.title: 통화 후 녹음 배포
useCases.description: Zoom 통화가 종료되고 클라우드 녹음이 처리되면 GAIA는 녹음 링크를 Google Calendar 이벤트에 추가하여 참석자가 원본 캘린더 항목을 확인하여 녹음을 찾을 수 있도록 합니다.

useCases.title: 캘린더 이벤트 유형별 미팅 분석
useCases.description: GAIA는 캘린더 이벤트 범주에 대해 Zoom 미팅 시간 및 참석률을 추적하여, 예약된 것보다 미팅 유형이 실제로 얼마나 많은 시간을 소비하는지에 대한 통찰력을 제공합니다.

howItWorks.step: Google Calendar 및 Zoom을 GAIA에 연결
howItWorks.description: Google Calendar 및 Zoom 계정에 GAIA를 인증하고 어떤 이벤트 유형이 자동으로 Zoom 링크를 얻어야 하는지 구성합니다.

howItWorks.step: 미팅 생성 기본 설정 설정
howItWorks.description: 미팅 카테고리별 대기실 사용, 중요한 미팅 자동 녹화, 호스트 비디오 기본 설정과 같이 이벤트 유형별 기본 Zoom 설정을 선택합니다.

howItWorks.step: Meetings run smoothly from start to finish
howItWorks.description: GAIA는 Zoom 링크 생성, 캘린더 이벤트 업데이트, 미팅 전 준비, 미팅 후 배포를 처리하므로, 대화에 완전히 집중할 수 있습니다.

faqs.question: GAIA는 미팅 생성 시 내 기존 Zoom 설정을 사용하나요(대기실, 비밀번호)?
faqs.answer: 네, GAIA는 계정의 기본 설정으로 Zoom 미팅을 생성합니다. 또한 이벤트 유형별 특정 설정을 구성할 수 있습니다 — 내부 팀 미팅이 아닌 외부 클라이언트 통화의 경우 대기실을 활성화하는 등.

faqs.question: GAIA는 Zoom뿐만 아니라 Google Meet와 함께 작동하나요?
faqs.answer: 둘 다 지원합니다. 참석자 도메인 또는 캘린더 태그를 기반으로 외부 미팅은 Zoom을 사용하고 내부 미팅은 Google Meet를 사용하도록 GAIA를 구성할 수 있습니다.

faqs.question: GAIA는 Zoom 미팅 생성 또는 업데이트 시 모든 참석자에게 알림을 보내나요?
faqs.answer: Google Calendar는 이벤트 업데이트에 대한 참석자 알림을 처리합니다. GAIA는 Zoom 세부 정보를 캘린더 이벤트에 업데이트하고 Google Calendar의 표준 초청 플로우는 자동으로 참석자에게 알림을 보냅니다.

todoist-asana.tagline: 개인 Todoist 작업을 팀 Asana 프로젝트와 원활하게 연결
todoist-asana.metaTitle: Todoist + Asana 자동화 - 개인 작업과 팀 작업 동기화 | GAIA
todoist-asana.metaDescription: GAIA로 Todoist 및 Asana를 연결합니다. Asana 할당을 Todoist에 동기화하고, Todoist 완료를 Asana에 동기화하며, 단일 보기에서 개인 및 팀 작업을 관리합니다.
todoist-asana.intro: 많은 전문가들은 개인 작업 관리에 Todoist를 사용하고 팀 프로젝트 작업에 Asana를 사용하지만, 이 두 시스템은 서로 통신하지 않습니다. Asana 작업 할당은 Todoist 받은 편지함에 나타나지 않습니다. Todoist의 개인 작업은 Asana 프로젝트 약속을 반영하지 않습니다. 결과적으로 두 개의 별도 시스템을 유지해야 합니다. GAIA는 개인 및 팀 작업 관리를 연결합니다. Asana에서 작업이 할당되면 Todoist에 나타납니다. Todoist에서 Asana에 매핑된 작업을 완료하면 둘 다 업데이트됩니다. 개인 생산성 시스템과 팀 프로젝트 관리가 이중 입력 없이 동기화 상태를 유지합니다.

useCases.title: Asana 할당을 Todoist로 동기화
useCases.description: Asana에서 작업이 할당되면 GAIA는 해당 작업을 개인 Todoist 받은 편지함에 자동으로 추가하므로, 모든 작업(개인 및 팀)이 하나의 작업 관리자에 나타납니다.

useCases.title: Todoist 완료를 Asana로 업데이트
useCases.description: Todoist에서 Asana 소스 작업을 완료하면 GAIA는 Asana에서 자동으로 완료로 표시하므로, 팀 프로젝트 보드가 추가 노력 없이 정확하게 유지됩니다.

useCases.title: Asana의 개인 작업 컨텍스트
useCases.description: Todoist personal tasks related to Asana projects can be linked so project managers see the full picture of work being done — both the tracked Asana tasks and the personal prep work in Todoist.

useCases.title: 마감일 동기화
useCases.description: Asana 작업 마감일이 변경되면 GAIA는 해당 Todoist 작업 마감일을 자동으로 업데이트하므로, 개인 우선순위 목록이 항상 최신 프로젝트 일정을 반영합니다.

useCases.title: Asana의 일일 작업 브리핑
useCases.description: 매일 아침 GAIA는 오늘 마감된 Asana 작업과 개인 Todoist 작업을 포함하는 개요를 생성하여, 하루의 약속에 대한 완전한 보기를 제공합니다.

howItWorks.step: Todoist 및 Asana를 GAIA에 연결
howItWorks.description: Todoist 계정 및 Asana 작업 공간에 GAIA를 인증합니다. 어떤 Asana 프로젝트에서 작업을 Todoist로 동기화해야 하는지 구성합니다.

howItWorks.step: 동기화 기본 설정 설정
howItWorks.description: 모든 Asana 할당이 Todoist로 동기화되는지 또는 특정 프로젝트의 할당만 동기화되는지 선택합니다. Asana 작업을 수신할 Todoist 프로젝트를 구성합니다.

howItWorks.step: One task list for all your work
howItWorks.description: GAIA maintains synchronization between both systems. You manage your work from Todoist while Asana stays updated for your team automatically.

faqs.question: 작업이 Todoist와 Asana 모두에 있는 경우 GAIA는 중복을 생성하나요?
faqs.answer: GAIA는 Asana에서 시작된 Todoist 작업을 추적하고 중복을 생성하지 않습니다. 업데이트는 새 항목을 만드는 대신 연결된 작업 간에 흐릅니다.

faqs.question: 개인 작업과 함께 Asana 작업을 구성하기 위해 Todoist 라벨을 사용할 수 있나요?
faqs.answer: 네, GAIA는 작업이มาจาก Asana 프로젝트에 따라 Todoist 라벨을 적용할 수 있으므로, Asana 작업만 표시하도록 Todoist 보기를 필터링하는 것이 쉽습니다.

faqs.question: 이것이 공유 Todoist 프로젝트와 함께 작동하나요, 아니면 개인 프로젝트만 작동하나요?
faqs.answer: 네, Asana 팀 작업을 공유 Todoist 프로젝트로 동기화할 수 있으므로, 선호하는 경우 팀 전체가 Todoist에서 Asana 작업을 관리할 수 있습니다.

asana-jira.tagline: Asana 프로젝트 작업을 Jira 엔지니어링 티켓과 자동으로 동기화
asana-jira.metaTitle: Asana + Jira 자동화 - 제품 및 엔지니어링 동기화 | GAIA
asana-jira.metaDescription: GAIA로 Asana 및 Jira를 자동화합니다. Asana 작업을 Jira 티켓과 동기화하고, 제품 및 엔지니어링을 정렬하며, 프로젝트 관리 도구 간의 수동 상태 업데이트를 제거합니다.
asana-jira.intro: 제품팀은 Asana에서 작업을 추적하고 엔지니어링 팀은 Jira에서 작업합니다. 둘 사이의 간격은 프로젝트가 느려지는 곳입니다. 제품 관리자는 Jira 티켓이 필요한 Asana 작업을 생성하고, 상태 업데이트는 두 도구 모두에 수동으로 미러링되어야 하며, 어느 쪽도 상대방에 대한 완전한 가시성을 갖지 못합니다. GAIA는 Jira와 Asana를 동기화하여 올바른 정보가 이중 입력 없이 두 곳에 모두 있도록 합니다. Asana 작업은 Jira 티켓을 생성합니다. Jira 상태 변경은 Asana를 업데이트합니다. 제품 관리자는 Asana에서 엔지니어링 진행 상황을 보고, 엔지니어는 Jira에서 제품 컨텍스트를 봅니다. 두 팀 모두 선호하는 도구에서 작업하지만 GAIA는 정렬합니다.

useCases.title: Asana 작업을 Jira 티켓으로 만들기
useCases.description: Asana 작업에 엔지니어링 작업이 필요한 경우 GAIA는 해당 Jira 티켓을 해당 제품 컨텍스트, 수락 기준 및 마감일로 생성하여 엔지니어링 팀을 지원합니다.

useCases.title: Jira 상태를 Asana에 미러링
useCases.description: Jira 티켓이 엔지니어링 워크플로우 단계를 진행함에 따라 GAIA는 연결된 Asana 작업 상태를 업데이트하므로 제품 관리자가 Jira를 확인하지 않고도 실시간 엔지니어링 진행 상황을 볼 수 있습니다.

useCases.title: Asana 마감일을 Jira에 알림
useCases.description: Asana 작업에 제품 마감일이 있는 경우 GAIA는 해당 마감일 컨텍스트를 연결된 Jira 티켓에 추가하여 엔지니어가 기술 작업 뒤에 있는 비즈니스 긴급성을 이해하도록 합니다.

useCases.title: Jira에서 Asana로 버그 에스컬레이션
useCases.description: UAT 또는 고객 피드백 검토 중에 Asana에서 버그가 플래그 지정되면 GAIA는 제품 팀의 프로젝트에 Asana 작업을 자동으로 생성하므로 제품 관리자가 고객 대면 영향을 추적하고 응답 통신을 조정할 수 있습니다.

useCases.title: 릴리스 계획 조정
useCases.description: Jira 버전이 릴리스되면 GAIA는 계획된 릴리스 작업의 몇 퍼센트가 Jira에서 완료되었는지 보고하기 위해 연결된 모든 Jira 티켓을 형식화된 릴리스 노트로 컴파일하여 제품 팀에 정확한 출시 신뢰도를 제공합니다.

howItWorks.step: Asana 및 Jira를 GAIA에 연결
howItWorks.description: Asana 작업 공간 및 Jira 프로젝트에 GAIA를 인증합니다. Asana 프로젝트와 Jira 프로젝트 간의 매핑을 정의합니다.

howItWorks.step: 양방향 동기화 규칙 구성
howItWorks.description: 어떤 Asana 작업 이벤트가 Jira 티켓을 생성하는지, 어떤 Jira 상태 변경이 Asana를 업데이트하는지, 시스템 간에 필드가 어떻게 매핑되는지 설정합니다.

howItWorks.step: Product and engineering work in their preferred tools
howItWorks.description: GAIA handles all status synchronization automatically. Both teams get the visibility they need without using each other's tools.

faqs.question: GAIA는 Asana 사용자 정의 필드를 Jira 사용자 정의 필드에 매핑할 수 있나요?
faqs.answer: 네, Asana의 사용자 정의 필드는 Jira의 사용자 정의 필드에 매핑될 수 있으므로 특수 메타데이터(예: 클라이언트 이름, 예산 코드 또는 구성 요소 영역)는 도구 간 작업 동기화 시 보존됩니다.

faqs.question: Asana 작업과 Jira 티켓이 동시에 업데이트되면 어떻게 되나요?
faqs.answer: GAIA는 타임스탬프 기반 충돌 해결을 사용하며 가장 최근에 수정된 업데이트를 신뢰할 수 있는 출처로 적용합니다. 중요한 필드의 경우 충돌 시 우선순위를 갖는 시스템을 구성할 수 있습니다.

faqs.question: 이것이 Asana 포트폴리오와 함께 작동하나요?
faqs.answer: 네, GAIA는 모든 포함된 프로젝트의 상태를 집계하는 Notion 페이지를 Asana 포트폴리오에 대해 생성할 수 있으므로 리더십은 Notion에서 포트폴리오 수준의 보기를 확보할 수 있습니다.

jira-linear.tagline: Sync Jira and Linear so teams using both stay perfectly aligned
jira-linear.metaTitle: Jira + Linear Automation - Cross-Team Issue Sync | GAIA
jira-linear.metaDescription: Automate Jira and Linear with GAIA. Sync issues between Jira and Linear, keep cross-team projects aligned, and eliminate the friction of managing work across two engineering tools.
jira-linear.intro:
Many organizations have teams that use Jira and teams that use Linear. Enterprise departments use Jira for compliance and audit trails while product engineering teams prefer Linear for speed. When these teams collaborate, work coordination requires manual translation between the two systems — a constant overhead that slows cross-team projects.

GAIA synchronizes Jira and Linear so cross-team work is visible in both places. Issues created in Linear for a cross-functional feature appear in Jira for the enterprise team. Jira ticket status changes flow back to Linear. Teams use their preferred tool while GAIA keeps the shared work in sync.

useCases.title: Cross-team issue visibility
useCases.description: When a Linear team creates an issue that requires input from a Jira team (like infrastructure or security), GAIA creates a linked Jira ticket so the work is tracked in both systems.

useCases.title: Bidirectional status sync
useCases.description: Issue status changes in either Jira or Linear propagate to the linked issue in the other system, so both teams always see current status without checking the other tool.

useCases.title: Migration assistance
useCases.description: Teams migrating from Jira to Linear use GAIA to run both tools in parallel during transition, keeping historical Jira issues linked to new Linear counterparts without losing audit history.

useCases.title: Dependency tracking across tools
useCases.description: When a Linear issue is blocked by a Jira ticket, GAIA links the dependency and notifies both teams so blockers are visible regardless of which tool each team checks.

useCases.title: Executive reporting across both platforms
useCases.description: GAIA compiles status from both Jira and Linear into a unified report for leadership, showing project health across teams without requiring executives to access either platform directly.

howItWorks.step: Connect Jira and Linear to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Jira instance and Linear workspace. Map which Jira projects correspond to which Linear teams for routing.

howItWorks.step: Configure sync rules and field mappings
howItWorks.description: Define which issue types sync between systems, how priority and status fields map across the two tools, and which events trigger cross-system updates.

howItWorks.step: Teams work independently, GAIA keeps them aligned
howItWorks.description: Each team works in their preferred tool. GAIA handles all cross-tool synchronization automatically so collaboration happens without tool-switching overhead.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle the different priority scales between Jira and Linear?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA maps Jira's priority levels (Highest, High, Medium, Low, Lowest) to Linear's priority scale (Urgent, High, Medium, Low, No priority) intelligently, and you can customize the mapping.

faqs.question: Does GAIA sync comments between Jira and Linear?
faqs.answer: Yes. Comments added to issues in either tool sync to the linked issue in the other system, so conversation context is preserved regardless of where team members prefer to comment.

faqs.question: Is this suitable for compliance-heavy Jira workflows?
faqs.answer: GAIA respects Jira's audit and compliance requirements. It creates issues and adds comments through the standard Jira API so all changes are properly attributed and logged in Jira's audit trail.

jira-github.tagline: Link Jira issues to GitHub PRs and keep them synchronized automatically
jira-github.metaTitle: Jira + GitHub Automation - Issue and Code Sync | GAIA
jira-github.metaDescription: Automate Jira and GitHub with GAIA. Sync Jira issues with GitHub pull requests, auto-update issue status when PRs merge, and give your team real-time development visibility in Jira.
jira-github.intro:
Jira tracks what needs to be built. GitHub is where it gets built. But the two rarely reflect the same state — PRs merge without the Jira issue updating, branches stay open after issues close, and development progress is invisible to product stakeholders who track everything in Jira.

GAIA automates the Jira-GitHub connection so both systems reflect engineering reality. PR events update Jira issues. Merged PRs transition issues to Done. New Jira issues can trigger branch creation. Your project board accurately reflects your codebase state without anyone manually updating it.

useCases.title: Automatic Jira issue transitions from PR events
useCases.description: When a GitHub PR referencing a Jira issue is opened, GAIA moves the issue to 'In Progress'. When the PR is merged, GAIA moves it to 'Done'. Jira always reflects the current development state.

useCases.title: GitHub branch creation from Jira issues
useCases.description: When a Jira issue moves to 'In Progress', GAIA creates the corresponding GitHub branch with the correct naming convention, saving engineers the manual branch setup step.

useCases.title: PR status in Jira comments
useCases.description: GAIA posts GitHub PR details (title, author, review status, CI status) as Jira issue comments so product managers and QA can see development progress directly in Jira.

useCases.title: Release notes from Jira to GitHub
useCases.description: GAIA compiles all Jira issues resolved in a sprint into formatted GitHub release notes, linking each changelog item back to the Jira issue for full traceability.

useCases.title: CI failure alerts on Jira issues
useCases.description: When GitHub Actions CI fails on a branch linked to a Jira issue, GAIA comments on the Jira issue with the failure details so the assigned engineer sees it in their issue tracker.

howItWorks.step: Connect Jira and GitHub to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Jira project and GitHub organization. Configure which repositories link to which Jira projects.

howItWorks.step: Set up the issue-PR linking convention
howItWorks.description: Define how PRs reference Jira issues — by issue key in the PR title, branch name, or description. Configure the Jira status transitions that correspond to each GitHub event.

howItWorks.step: Code and project management stay in sync
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors both systems and keeps them synchronized. Engineers work in GitHub, stakeholders track in Jira, and both see the same reality.

faqs.question: How does GAIA identify which Jira issue a GitHub PR relates to?
faqs.answer: GAIA looks for Jira issue keys (like PROJECT-123) in PR titles, branch names, and PR descriptions. This convention is standard practice at most engineering teams and requires no extra tooling.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle GitHub PRs that close multiple Jira issues?
faqs.answer: Yes. If a PR references multiple Jira issue keys, GAIA updates all linked issues when the PR status changes. Each issue is updated individually with the appropriate transition.

faqs.question: Does GAIA's integration complement or replace Jira's native GitHub app?
faqs.answer: GAIA provides more intelligent automation than Jira's native GitHub integration. It handles smart status transitions, CI notifications, and branch creation that the native integration doesn't support.

zoom-google-calendar.tagline: Automatically add Zoom links to Google Calendar events and manage meetings effortlessly
zoom-google-calendar.metaTitle: Zoom + Google Calendar Automation - Video Meeting Management | GAIA
zoom-google-calendar.metaDescription: Automate Zoom and Google Calendar with GAIA. Generate Zoom links for calendar events automatically, receive pre-meeting reminders, and manage your video meeting schedule without manual effort.
zoom-google-calendar.intro:
Every external meeting involves the same repetitive steps: create a Zoom meeting, copy the link, edit the calendar event, paste the link, and hope nothing changes. When meetings get rescheduled or new attendees are added, the process repeats. This low-value administrative work takes time away from actual meeting preparation.

GAIA automates the Zoom and Google Calendar connection. Calendar events automatically get Zoom meetings created and linked. Reschedules update Zoom meetings without manual intervention. Pre-meeting reminders arrive with working join links and context. The administrative overhead of video meeting management disappears.

useCases.title: Automatic Zoom links for calendar events
useCases.description: When you create a Google Calendar event with external attendees, GAIA generates a Zoom meeting and adds the join URL, meeting ID, and passcode to the event description automatically.

useCases.title: Meeting reminders with context
useCases.description: Ten minutes before each Zoom-linked calendar event, GAIA sends a reminder with the join link, attendee list, any shared agenda, and a summary of recent interactions with the attendees.

useCases.title: Reschedule Zoom meetings with calendar
useCases.description: When a Google Calendar event is rescheduled, GAIA updates the corresponding Zoom meeting time automatically so attendees always have a working join link with current timing.

useCases.title: Post-meeting recording distribution
useCases.description: After a Zoom call ends and the cloud recording processes, GAIA adds the recording link to the Google Calendar event so attendees can find the recording by checking the original calendar entry.

useCases.title: Zoom meeting analytics by calendar event type
useCases.description: GAIA tracks Zoom meeting duration and attendance against calendar event categories, providing insights into how much time different meeting types actually consume versus what was scheduled.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Google Calendar to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Google Calendar. Configure which calendar event types should automatically get Zoom meetings created.

howItWorks.step: Set your meeting creation preferences
howItWorks.description: Choose default Zoom settings per event type — waiting rooms for client calls, auto-recording for important meetings, and host video preferences by meeting category.

howItWorks.step: Meetings are managed end to end automatically
howItWorks.description: From calendar event creation through post-meeting recording distribution, GAIA handles all the administrative work of running Zoom meetings via your calendar.

faqs.question: Does GAIA create a new Zoom meeting for every calendar event or reuse links?
faqs.answer: By default GAIA creates a unique Zoom meeting per calendar event for security and tracking. For recurring events, you can configure GAIA to use a persistent Zoom room instead.

faqs.question: Can GAIA manage Zoom webinars in addition to regular meetings?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can create Zoom webinars from calendar events for large external events and manage registration, reminders, and post-webinar recording distribution.

faqs.question: What happens to the Zoom meeting if a calendar event is cancelled?
faqs.answer: GAIA automatically cancels the corresponding Zoom meeting when a Google Calendar event is deleted, preventing orphaned Zoom meetings and keeping your Zoom account tidy.

zoom-slack.tagline: Start Zoom calls from Slack and share meeting summaries automatically
zoom-slack.metaTitle: Zoom + Slack Automation - Video Meetings from Chat | GAIA
zoom-slack.metaDescription: Automate Zoom and Slack with GAIA. Start Zoom meetings from Slack, share meeting summaries to channels, get pre-meeting reminders in chat, and keep your team coordinated across both tools.
zoom-slack.intro:
Teams coordinate on Slack but meet on Zoom. The switch between the two creates friction — finding the Zoom link, notifying the team a meeting is starting, sharing what was decided after the call. GAIA connects Zoom and Slack so video meetings flow naturally from chat and back again.

With GAIA, starting a Zoom call from Slack takes one message. Meeting summaries post to the right channels automatically after calls end. Pre-meeting reminders arrive in Slack with all the context you need. The transition between Slack conversation and Zoom meeting becomes invisible.

useCases.title: Start Zoom meetings from Slack
useCases.description: Ask GAIA in any Slack channel to start a Zoom meeting for the conversation. GAIA creates the Zoom room and posts the join link to the channel so participants can join instantly.

useCases.title: Post meeting summaries to Slack
useCases.description: After a Zoom call, GAIA posts a structured summary to the relevant Slack channel including key decisions, action items, and participants so the team is aligned even if they missed the call.

useCases.title: Zoom meeting reminders in Slack
useCases.description: Before scheduled Zoom meetings linked to your calendar, GAIA sends a Slack DM with the join link, agenda, and attendee list so you're prepared and on time.

useCases.title: Recording notifications to Slack
useCases.description: When a Zoom cloud recording is ready, GAIA posts the recording link to the designated Slack channel so team members who missed the live call can catch up immediately.

useCases.title: On-call meeting triggers
useCases.description: When an incident alert fires in Slack, GAIA automatically creates an incident Zoom room and posts the join link to the on-call channel so the team can join a war room instantly.

howItWorks.step: Connect Zoom and Slack to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authorize GAIA with your Zoom account and Slack workspace. Configure which Slack channels should receive meeting summaries and recording notifications.

howItWorks.step: Configure meeting triggers and summary preferences
howItWorks.description: Choose what meeting summaries include, which channels get notified for different meeting types, and how pre-meeting reminders should be formatted.

howItWorks.step: Chat and video meetings work as one system
howItWorks.description: Zoom meetings start from Slack effortlessly. Post-meeting content flows back into Slack automatically. Your team's communication stays unified across both tools.

faqs.question: Can GAIA identify which Slack channel to post a meeting summary to?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA uses the channel where the meeting was initiated, the calendar event description, or rules you configure (like 'engineering Zoom calls post to #engineering-updates') to route summaries intelligently.

faqs.question: Does GAIA require Zoom cloud recording to generate meeting summaries?
faqs.answer: Zoom cloud recording with transcription provides the richest summaries. Without it, GAIA generates summaries from calendar event context and any pre/post meeting Slack messages. Results improve significantly with transcription access.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle recurring Zoom meetings with the same Slack channel?
faqs.answer: Yes. Recurring meetings can be configured to always post summaries and recordings to the same Slack channel, building a running archive of meeting outcomes in one place.

gmail-github.tagline: Turn emails into GitHub issues and route PR alerts to your inbox
gmail-github.metaTitle: Gmail + GitHub Automation - Email to Issues, PR Alerts | GAIA
gmail-github.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and GitHub with GAIA. Create issues from emails, get pull request notifications in your inbox, and keep engineering and stakeholders aligned automatically.
gmail-github.intro:
Bug reports arrive by email. Feature requests come from clients over email threads. Yet your engineering workflow lives entirely in GitHub. The gap between these two worlds creates a tedious manual loop: copy the email, open GitHub, create an issue, paste the details, and remember to update both sides when progress is made.

GAIA eliminates this translation layer by connecting Gmail and GitHub directly. When a client emails a bug report or a stakeholder sends a feature request, GAIA can create a properly formatted GitHub issue with the right labels, assignees, and milestones extracted from the email context. Conversely, when pull requests are opened, reviewed, or merged, GAIA can route structured summaries to relevant inboxes so non-technical stakeholders stay informed without needing GitHub access.

This integration is particularly valuable for teams where product managers, client-facing staff, and engineers need to collaborate across the email-to-code divide without losing context or creating duplicated effort.

useCases.title: Create GitHub issues from bug report emails
useCases.description: When a client or teammate emails a bug report, GAIA parses the email, extracts the key details — steps to reproduce, environment, severity — and creates a formatted GitHub issue in the correct repository with appropriate labels. The original sender is referenced in the issue body so engineers have full context.

useCases.title: Feature request pipeline from email to backlog
useCases.description: Product feedback and feature requests sent by email are automatically converted into GitHub issues tagged as feature requests and added to the relevant project backlog. GAIA extracts the core request and formats it as a user story so engineers can act on it immediately.

useCases.title: PR status notifications for non-technical stakeholders
useCases.description: When a pull request tied to a client-facing feature is opened, reviewed, or merged, GAIA sends a plain-language email summary to the relevant stakeholders so they know progress is being made without needing to navigate GitHub.

useCases.title: Release notification emails
useCases.description: When a new GitHub release is published, GAIA composes and sends a formatted release summary email to your distribution list — including changelog highlights, known issues, and upgrade notes — keeping clients and internal teams informed automatically.

useCases.title: Email-triggered issue comments
useCases.description: When a stakeholder replies to a GitHub notification email with a comment or update, GAIA posts their reply as a comment on the original GitHub issue so the conversation stays unified in the engineering workflow.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and GitHub to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and GitHub organization or personal account in GAIA's integration settings. GAIA uses OAuth for both connections so your credentials remain secure.

howItWorks.step: Configure your routing rules
howItWorks.description: Tell GAIA which email senders, labels, or keywords should trigger issue creation, which repositories to target, and which GitHub events should send email notifications. You can specify assignees, labels, and milestones as part of the rules.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automates the email-to-GitHub workflow
howItWorks.description: Once configured, GAIA monitors Gmail and GitHub continuously, creating issues from qualifying emails and dispatching notifications for relevant GitHub events without any manual intervention.

faqs.question: Can GAIA create issues in specific repositories based on email content?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can set up rules that route emails to different repositories based on sender, subject keywords, Gmail labels, or AI classification of the email content. For example, emails about your mobile app can go to the mobile-app repo while API-related emails go to the backend repo.

faqs.question: Will GAIA avoid creating duplicate issues if the same bug is emailed twice?
faqs.answer: GAIA checks for existing open issues with similar titles and content before creating a new one. If a likely duplicate is found, GAIA can add the new email as a comment on the existing issue instead, keeping discussions consolidated.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle GitHub notifications I already receive in Gmail?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can parse GitHub's native notification emails and enrich them — summarizing long PR review threads, extracting action items, or routing specific notifications to teammates — rather than just letting them accumulate in your inbox.

gmail-trello.tagline: Convert emails into Trello cards and keep your boards updated from your inbox
gmail-trello.metaTitle: Gmail + Trello Automation - Email to Cards, Inbox to Board | GAIA
gmail-trello.metaDescription: Automate Gmail and Trello with GAIA. Create Trello cards from emails, update board lists based on email replies, and manage tasks without switching between your inbox and boards.
gmail-trello.intro:
Email is where work requests arrive; Trello is where work gets organized and tracked. But bridging the two requires constant manual effort — reading emails, deciding which board and list they belong in, creating cards, copying over the relevant details, and then returning to Gmail to reply. For teams managing high email volumes, this becomes a significant time drain that takes attention away from actual work.

GAIA connects Gmail and Trello so that email content flows directly into your boards. Incoming emails that represent tasks, requests, or deliverables can be automatically converted into Trello cards with the right metadata, assigned to the correct list, and linked back to the original email thread. When card statuses change in Trello, GAIA can notify the relevant people via email so external stakeholders stay informed without needing Trello access.

This integration is ideal for client-facing teams, project managers, and operations teams who live in both email and Trello and want to eliminate the manual handoff between the two.

useCases.title: Create Trello cards from client request emails
useCases.description: When a client emails a new request, GAIA creates a Trello card in the appropriate board and list, pre-populated with the request details, due date if mentioned, and a link back to the original email thread so your team has full context when they pick up the card.

useCases.title: Move cards based on email replies
useCases.description: When you or a teammate sends an email update on a project, GAIA can move the corresponding Trello card to the next list — for example, from 'In Progress' to 'Awaiting Client Feedback' — so your board reflects the actual state of work without manual updates.

useCases.title: Email notifications when cards are completed
useCases.description: When a Trello card is moved to 'Done', GAIA composes and sends a completion notification email to the relevant stakeholder, summarizing what was done and any next steps, keeping clients informed without requiring manual follow-up emails.

useCases.title: Daily board digest to email
useCases.description: GAIA sends a morning email digest summarizing the state of your Trello boards — cards due today, overdue items, and recently completed tasks — so you start the day with a clear picture of priorities without opening Trello.

useCases.title: Attachment forwarding from email to cards
useCases.description: Files and documents sent as email attachments are automatically attached to the corresponding Trello card so your team has everything they need in one place without hunting through email threads for assets.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Trello to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and Trello workspace in GAIA's integration settings. GAIA will ask which boards it should have access to so you control the scope of the integration.

howItWorks.step: Set up your card creation rules
howItWorks.description: Tell GAIA which types of emails should become Trello cards, which board and list they should land in, how to set labels and due dates, and who to assign cards to. Rules can be based on sender, Gmail labels, keywords, or AI classification.

howItWorks.step: GAIA manages the email-to-board pipeline automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors your Gmail and Trello boards continuously, creating cards from qualifying emails and sending notifications for board events according to your rules — no manual copying required.

faqs.question: Can GAIA create cards on different Trello boards based on the email content?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can set rules that route emails to different boards based on sender domain, Gmail labels, or AI-detected content type. For example, design request emails go to your Design board while support emails go to your Support board.

faqs.question: What happens to the original email after GAIA creates a Trello card?
faqs.answer: GAIA can apply a Gmail label such as 'Sent to Trello' to the original email and optionally archive it so your inbox stays clean. The link back to the email thread is stored in the Trello card description so you can always find the source.

faqs.question: Can I create a Trello card manually by forwarding an email to GAIA?
faqs.answer: Yes. In addition to automatic rules, you can forward any email to GAIA with a quick instruction like 'add this to the Marketing board under To Do' and GAIA will create the card immediately.

gmail-discord.tagline: Route important emails to Discord channels and keep your community in the loop
gmail-discord.metaTitle: Gmail + Discord Automation - Email Notifications to Discord | GAIA
gmail-discord.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and Discord with GAIA. Forward important email alerts to Discord channels, notify your community about key updates, and bridge email communications with Discord automatically.
gmail-discord.intro:
Many teams and communities coordinate in Discord but still receive critical notifications — system alerts, client emails, payment confirmations, and vendor communications — via Gmail. Manually monitoring both platforms creates alert fatigue and gaps in awareness, especially for distributed teams spread across time zones.

GAIA bridges Gmail and Discord by routing important email notifications to the right Discord channels automatically. System alert emails can post to a #monitoring channel. Client communication summaries can land in a #clients channel. Financial notifications can appear in a #finance channel. Your Discord server becomes a real-time nerve center that pulls in email-based signals without anyone having to relay them manually.

This is especially useful for developer communities, open-source projects, gaming studios, and creator-led businesses that run their operations inside Discord but still interact with partners and vendors over email.

useCases.title: System alert emails to Discord monitoring channel
useCases.description: Infrastructure monitoring tools often send alert emails when systems go down or thresholds are breached. GAIA routes these emails to your #monitoring Discord channel the moment they arrive, with a formatted summary so your on-call team can respond immediately without checking email.

useCases.title: Client update summaries to team Discord
useCases.description: When important client emails arrive in Gmail, GAIA posts a concise summary to the relevant Discord channel so the whole team is aware of client needs and context without being CC'd on every email thread.

useCases.title: Payment and billing notifications
useCases.description: Payment confirmations, subscription renewals, and billing alerts from services like Stripe or PayPal arrive by email. GAIA forwards these to a private Discord channel for your finance or operations team so nothing is missed.

useCases.title: Community announcement emails to Discord
useCases.description: When you send a newsletter or announcement to your email list, GAIA can simultaneously post a version to your Discord announcements channel so your Discord community receives the news at the same time as your email subscribers.

useCases.title: New lead or inquiry alerts
useCases.description: When a contact form submission or sales inquiry arrives in Gmail, GAIA posts an alert to your #sales or #leads Discord channel so the team can discuss and assign follow-up immediately rather than waiting for someone to check email.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Discord to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and authorize GAIA to post to your Discord server. GAIA uses Discord webhooks or bot permissions to post to specific channels, so you control exactly where messages appear.

howItWorks.step: Map email types to Discord channels
howItWorks.description: Define rules for which emails should route to which Discord channels. You can filter by sender, subject keywords, Gmail labels, or let GAIA use AI classification to determine the appropriate channel.

howItWorks.step: GAIA monitors Gmail and posts to Discord automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA watches your inbox continuously and posts formatted notifications to the configured Discord channels as qualifying emails arrive, keeping your Discord server updated in real time.

faqs.question: Can GAIA format the Discord message differently from the original email?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can post the full email, an AI-generated summary, or a custom template that extracts specific fields like sender, subject, and key points. For noisy alert emails, a concise one-line summary is often more useful than the full email body.

faqs.question: Can GAIA post to multiple Discord channels for the same email?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can configure rules where a single email triggers posts to multiple channels. For example, a major client email might post to both #clients and #management so both teams are notified simultaneously.

faqs.question: Will GAIA work with private Discord channels?
faqs.answer: Yes, as long as the GAIA bot or webhook has permission to post in the private channel. During setup, you grant GAIA access to specific channels, and it will only post where it is authorized.

gmail-drive.tagline: Automatically save email attachments and content to Google Drive
gmail-drive.metaTitle: Gmail + Google Drive Automation - Save Attachments Automatically | GAIA
gmail-drive.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and Google Drive with GAIA. Auto-save email attachments to Drive folders, convert email threads to Drive documents, and keep files organized without manual downloading.
gmail-drive.intro:
Email attachments are one of the most common ways files enter a team's workflow, yet they remain locked inside Gmail threads rather than organized in a shared Drive folder where everyone can find them. Downloading attachments manually, renaming them, and uploading them to the right folder is a repetitive chore that adds up to hours of wasted time each week.

GAIA automates the entire pipeline from Gmail to Google Drive. Attachments from qualifying emails are saved to the correct Drive folders automatically, named consistently, and organized by sender, project, or date depending on your preferences. Entire email threads can be converted into Drive documents for archiving. Contracts, invoices, and reports that arrive by email end up exactly where your team expects to find them.

This integration is invaluable for finance teams handling invoices, legal teams receiving contracts, operations teams managing vendor documents, and any team that regularly receives files by email and needs them accessible in a shared Drive.

useCases.title: Auto-save invoice attachments to the correct Drive folder
useCases.description: When an invoice or receipt arrives in Gmail, GAIA extracts the attachment and saves it to the designated Drive folder for that vendor or project. The file is named consistently — including vendor name, invoice number, and date — so your accounting team can always find it without searching email.

useCases.title: Contract and agreement archiving
useCases.description: Signed contracts and legal agreements sent by email are automatically saved to your Contracts Drive folder. GAIA can also create a Drive shortcut linked back to the original email thread for full context.

useCases.title: Project asset collection
useCases.description: Design files, briefs, and project assets sent by clients or vendors over email are automatically routed to the correct project folder in Drive so your team has access without anyone acting as a manual file relay.

useCases.title: Email thread to Drive document
useCases.description: Important email threads — project decisions, client approvals, or policy discussions — can be converted by GAIA into formatted Drive documents for long-term archiving and easy sharing with people who weren't on the original thread.

useCases.title: Photo and media attachment organization
useCases.description: Photos and media files emailed by clients or partners are saved to organized Drive folders automatically, eliminating the need to manually download and re-upload assets that arrive in your inbox.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Google Drive to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Google account for both Gmail and Drive access. GAIA uses a single OAuth connection for Google Workspace services, so you only authorize once and specify which Drive folders GAIA should have write access to.

howItWorks.step: Configure your folder routing rules
howItWorks.description: Tell GAIA which types of attachments or emails should go to which Drive folders. You can base rules on sender domain, Gmail labels, subject keywords, file type, or AI-detected document category such as invoice, contract, or design file.

howItWorks.step: GAIA automatically saves files as they arrive
howItWorks.description: From the moment setup is complete, GAIA monitors incoming emails and saves qualifying attachments to Drive without any manual action. Files are organized, named, and placed exactly where your team expects them.

faqs.question: Can GAIA organize files into subfolders based on the email sender or date?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can instruct GAIA to create a folder structure based on sender name, sender domain, year and month, project name, or any combination. For example, invoices can be organized into Drive folders by vendor and then by month automatically.

faqs.question: What file types can GAIA save to Drive?
faqs.answer: GAIA can save any file type that Gmail supports as an attachment, including PDFs, Word documents, Excel spreadsheets, images, ZIP archives, and more. Google Workspace files like Docs and Sheets are handled natively.

faqs.question: Will GAIA create duplicate files if the same attachment is sent twice?
faqs.answer: GAIA checks for existing files with the same name and content hash before saving. If a duplicate is detected, it will skip the save or append a version suffix depending on your preference, keeping your Drive folders clean.

gmail-hubspot.tagline: Sync Gmail conversations with HubSpot CRM contacts and deals automatically
gmail-hubspot.metaTitle: Gmail + HubSpot Automation - Email to CRM, Contacts Synced | GAIA
gmail-hubspot.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and HubSpot with GAIA. Log email conversations to CRM contacts, create deals from emails, update contact records automatically, and never lose a sales interaction.
gmail-hubspot.intro:
Sales and customer success teams rely on HubSpot to manage relationships, but the actual conversations happen in Gmail. When email interactions aren't consistently logged to CRM, your HubSpot data becomes incomplete, deal timelines have gaps, and new team members lack context when they take over an account. Manual CRM logging is tedious enough that it gets skipped, especially under pressure.

GAIA bridges Gmail and HubSpot by keeping them synchronized automatically. When you exchange emails with a HubSpot contact, GAIA logs the conversation to the correct contact record, extracts action items, and updates deal stages based on email signals. New contacts discovered in email can be automatically created in HubSpot with relevant context. Your CRM stays accurate without relying on sales reps to remember to log every interaction.

This integration is essential for sales teams who do high-volume outreach, account managers who maintain long-term client relationships, and customer success teams who need a complete interaction history for every account.

useCases.title: Automatic email logging to HubSpot contacts
useCases.description: Every qualifying email you send or receive is automatically logged as an activity on the corresponding HubSpot contact record. The full email content, date, and thread context are captured so your CRM always reflects what has actually been communicated with each contact.

useCases.title: Create HubSpot deals from inbound email inquiries
useCases.description: When a prospect emails with an inquiry or request for information, GAIA creates a new HubSpot deal and associates it with the contact, setting the deal stage, estimated value if mentioned, and linked email thread so your sales team can follow up immediately from HubSpot.

useCases.title: Update deal stages based on email signals
useCases.description: When an email indicates a deal has progressed — a prospect confirms a meeting, sends a purchase order, or requests a contract — GAIA updates the HubSpot deal stage accordingly so your pipeline always reflects current reality without requiring manual updates.

useCases.title: New contact creation from email signatures
useCases.description: When you receive an email from someone not yet in HubSpot, GAIA extracts contact details from the email signature — name, company, phone, title — and creates a new HubSpot contact record enriched with that information.

useCases.title: Follow-up task creation from email commitments
useCases.description: When an email contains a commitment — 'I'll get back to you by Friday' or 'let's schedule a call next week' — GAIA creates a follow-up task in HubSpot assigned to the responsible rep so commitments are tracked and nothing falls through the cracks.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and HubSpot to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and HubSpot portal in GAIA. GAIA uses HubSpot's OAuth integration and requests only the permissions needed to read and write contacts, deals, and activities.

howItWorks.step: Define your sync rules and preferences
howItWorks.description: Specify which email addresses or domains should trigger CRM logging, how contacts should be matched or created, and which email signals indicate deal stage changes. You can also set which team members' inboxes GAIA should monitor.

howItWorks.step: GAIA keeps Gmail and HubSpot synchronized automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors qualifying inboxes and logs interactions to HubSpot continuously. Your CRM data stays current without manual entry, giving your whole team an accurate and complete view of every customer relationship.

faqs.question: Can GAIA match emails to existing HubSpot contacts automatically?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA matches emails to HubSpot contacts by email address first, and if no match is found, it can search by company domain. You can configure whether unmatched emails create new contacts automatically or are flagged for manual review.

faqs.question: Will personal emails also be logged to HubSpot?
faqs.answer: No. GAIA only logs emails that match your defined rules — typically emails from or to contacts in HubSpot or email addresses from known customer domains. Internal team emails and personal communications are excluded by default.

faqs.question: Can GAIA work across multiple team members' Gmail inboxes?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can be configured for an entire sales or customer success team, logging interactions from each team member's Gmail to the appropriate HubSpot contact records so your CRM captures the full picture of every customer relationship.

gmail-salesforce.tagline: Log emails to Salesforce CRM and create leads from your inbox automatically
gmail-salesforce.metaTitle: Gmail + Salesforce Automation - Email Logging, Lead Creation | GAIA
gmail-salesforce.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and Salesforce with GAIA. Automatically log emails to Salesforce records, create leads from inbound emails, update opportunities, and keep your CRM data accurate.
gmail-salesforce.intro:
Salesforce is the system of record for enterprise sales teams, but keeping it accurate requires logging every customer interaction — a task that falls on sales reps who are already stretched thin. Studies consistently show that CRM data quality suffers because manual logging is time-consuming, inconsistent, and the first thing to be skipped when reps are busy closing deals.

GAIA automates Gmail-to-Salesforce data flow so your CRM is kept accurate without burdening your sales team. Inbound emails from prospects automatically create Salesforce leads with the correct lead source, contact details, and email content captured. Existing customer email threads are logged to the right Account, Contact, and Opportunity records. Deal-progressing email signals trigger Opportunity stage updates. Your Salesforce data becomes a true reflection of sales activity rather than a manual approximation.

This integration is designed for enterprise sales teams, revenue operations managers who depend on CRM data quality, and organizations where Salesforce is the authoritative system of record for all customer interactions.

useCases.title: Automatic lead creation from inbound inquiry emails
useCases.description: When a prospect emails your sales team for the first time, GAIA creates a Salesforce Lead record with all available contact information extracted from the email and signature, the lead source set to 'Email', and the original email attached to the record so your sales reps have full context when following up.

useCases.title: Email activity logging to Accounts and Opportunities
useCases.description: Every email exchange with a Salesforce Contact is automatically logged as an Email Activity on the related Account and Opportunity records. Sales managers get a complete interaction timeline without chasing reps to log their calls and emails manually.

useCases.title: Opportunity stage advancement from email signals
useCases.description: When an email from a prospect contains language indicating deal progression — a request for a proposal, a reference check, or a verbal commitment — GAIA advances the associated Salesforce Opportunity to the appropriate stage and creates a follow-up task for the rep.

useCases.title: Contact record enrichment from email signatures
useCases.description: GAIA extracts contact details from email signatures — title, direct phone, LinkedIn profile, and company details — and updates the corresponding Salesforce Contact record so your CRM data stays enriched and current as relationships evolve.

useCases.title: Lead conversion email notifications
useCases.description: When a Salesforce Lead is converted to an Account and Contact after a successful qualification call, GAIA sends an automated welcome email from Gmail to the new contact, keeping communication warm while the Salesforce record is being set up.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Salesforce to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and Salesforce organization in GAIA. GAIA supports both Salesforce sandbox and production environments and requests only the API permissions needed for reading and writing Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities, and Activities.

howItWorks.step: Map your email-to-Salesforce rules
howItWorks.description: Configure which email senders trigger lead creation, how emails are matched to existing Salesforce records, which Opportunity stage changes map to which email signal keywords, and which team members' inboxes should be monitored.

howItWorks.step: GAIA keeps your Salesforce CRM automatically updated
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors qualifying Gmail inboxes and updates Salesforce records in real time as emails arrive and are sent. Your CRM stays current and your sales team spends more time selling and less time on data entry.

faqs.question: Does GAIA support Salesforce custom objects, not just standard ones?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can read and write to custom Salesforce objects in addition to standard Leads, Contacts, Accounts, and Opportunities. You can map email content to custom fields by describing the mapping in GAIA's configuration.

faqs.question: How does GAIA handle emails that match multiple Salesforce records?
faqs.answer: When an email matches multiple Salesforce records — for example, a contact associated with multiple opportunities — GAIA will log the activity to all relevant records or prompt for disambiguation based on your configuration preferences.

faqs.question: Is this compatible with Salesforce Lightning and Classic?
faqs.answer: GAIA integrates with Salesforce via the REST API, which is compatible with both Lightning and Classic. The integration works regardless of which Salesforce UI your team uses.

gmail-airtable.tagline: Save email data to Airtable databases and trigger workflows from your inbox
gmail-airtable.metaTitle: Gmail + Airtable Automation - Email Data to Airtable Records | GAIA
gmail-airtable.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and Airtable with GAIA. Automatically create Airtable records from emails, extract structured data from your inbox, and keep databases updated without manual data entry.
gmail-airtable.intro:
Airtable's power lies in its ability to structure and organize information flexibly — but feeding that database with data from email still requires someone to read each email and manually enter the relevant fields. For teams that receive high volumes of structured information by email — orders, applications, registrations, inquiries, or reports — manual data entry is a bottleneck that slows down operations and introduces errors.

GAIA connects Gmail and Airtable so that email content flows automatically into the right database fields. When an order confirmation arrives, GAIA creates an Airtable record with the order number, customer name, items, and value extracted from the email. When a job application lands in your inbox, GAIA creates an applicant record with name, contact details, and relevant experience parsed from the email. Your Airtable databases stay current without anyone acting as a manual data relay.

This is particularly powerful for operations teams running Airtable-based workflows, HR teams managing applicant tracking, event teams handling registrations, and any team that receives structured data via email and needs it organized in a database.

useCases.title: Order and purchase confirmation data capture
useCases.description: When order confirmation or purchase receipt emails arrive, GAIA extracts the structured data — order number, items, quantities, amounts, vendor name, and delivery date — and creates a new Airtable record in your orders or purchases database with all fields populated automatically.

useCases.title: Job application intake
useCases.description: When job applications arrive by email, GAIA parses the applicant's details, extracts the position they are applying for, and creates a new record in your Airtable applicant tracking base, assigning the appropriate status and notifying the hiring manager.

useCases.title: Event registration management
useCases.description: Registration confirmation emails from events, webinars, or workshops are parsed by GAIA and added as records in your Airtable events database with attendee name, company, role, and registration date captured automatically.

useCases.title: Vendor invoice tracking
useCases.description: Invoice emails from vendors are processed by GAIA, which extracts invoice number, vendor name, amount due, and payment due date, then creates a record in your Airtable accounts payable database and alerts your finance team if the amount exceeds a defined threshold.

useCases.title: Customer feedback collection
useCases.description: Feedback and survey responses received by email are parsed by GAIA and stored as structured records in your Airtable feedback database, categorized by sentiment and topic so your product team can analyze patterns without reading every email manually.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Airtable to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and Airtable workspace in GAIA. Specify which Airtable bases and tables GAIA should have write access to, and GAIA will map your email fields to Airtable columns.

howItWorks.step: Define your email-to-record mapping
howItWorks.description: Tell GAIA which types of emails should create records in which Airtable tables, and how email content maps to table fields. GAIA uses AI to extract structured data from unstructured email text, so you don't need perfectly formatted emails.

howItWorks.step: GAIA populates your Airtable databases automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA processes qualifying emails as they arrive and creates or updates Airtable records accordingly. Your databases stay current without manual data entry, and your team can focus on acting on the data rather than entering it.

faqs.question: Can GAIA update existing Airtable records as well as create new ones?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can match incoming emails to existing Airtable records based on a key field like email address or order number, and update the record rather than creating a duplicate. For example, a follow-up email from a job applicant can update their existing applicant record.

faqs.question: What if the email doesn't contain all the fields my Airtable table requires?
faqs.answer: GAIA will populate the fields it can extract from the email and leave required fields blank or set them to a default value you specify. You can configure GAIA to notify you when a record is created with missing required fields so you can complete it manually.

faqs.question: Can GAIA trigger Airtable automations after creating a record?
faqs.answer: Yes. Once GAIA creates a record in Airtable, any Airtable automations triggered by record creation will fire as normal. You can also configure GAIA to send a confirmation email or Slack notification after creating the Airtable record.

gmail-stripe.tagline: Enrich payment emails with Stripe data and automate revenue notifications
gmail-stripe.metaTitle: Gmail + Stripe Automation - Payment Emails, Revenue Alerts | GAIA
gmail-stripe.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and Stripe with GAIA. Enrich payment notification emails with Stripe customer data, automate revenue summaries, and act on billing events without manual lookups.
gmail-stripe.intro:
Stripe sends email notifications for payments, failed charges, disputes, and subscription events, but those emails contain only minimal context — a transaction ID and an amount. Getting the full picture requires logging into Stripe, finding the customer, and cross-referencing with your own records. For finance teams and operations managers processing dozens of payment events daily, this back-and-forth is a significant time drain.

GAIA connects Gmail and Stripe so that payment-related emails are automatically enriched with detailed Stripe customer and transaction data. When a payment failure email arrives, GAIA fetches the customer's full payment history, subscription status, and contact details and compiles a complete summary so your team can respond intelligently without opening Stripe. Dispute and chargeback emails trigger automated evidence gathering. Revenue milestone emails can be generated automatically based on Stripe data.

This integration is most valuable for SaaS companies, e-commerce teams, and subscription businesses where payment events require coordinated action from finance, customer success, and operations teams.

useCases.title: Payment failure enrichment and follow-up
useCases.description: When a failed payment notification arrives from Stripe, GAIA fetches the customer's full details from Stripe — their plan, payment history, and contact information — and composes a personalized follow-up email ready for review, so your team can respond in minutes rather than manually looking up each case.

useCases.title: Dispute and chargeback response automation
useCases.description: When a Stripe dispute notification email arrives, GAIA automatically pulls the relevant transaction details, order confirmation, and communication history from Gmail and assembles a dispute evidence package to streamline your response before the deadline.

useCases.title: Daily and weekly revenue summaries
useCases.description: GAIA pulls revenue data from Stripe and delivers a structured email summary to your finance team every morning or week — including total revenue, new subscribers, churned accounts, and outstanding invoices — eliminating the need for manual Stripe dashboard exports.

useCases.title: Subscription event notifications to team email
useCases.description: When a customer upgrades, downgrades, or cancels their subscription in Stripe, GAIA sends an enriched notification email to the relevant internal team with the customer's account history and context so customer success can reach out proactively.

useCases.title: High-value payment alerts
useCases.description: When a Stripe payment above a defined threshold is processed, GAIA sends an immediate alert email to your sales or finance leadership with the customer details, plan, and payment history so high-value transactions receive the attention they deserve.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Stripe to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and connect your Stripe account via API key in GAIA. GAIA uses read-only Stripe API access by default so your financial data remains secure.

howItWorks.step: Configure your payment event rules
howItWorks.description: Define which Stripe events should trigger email actions, what data to pull from Stripe for enrichment, who should receive notifications, and what thresholds apply for high-value or high-priority alerts.

howItWorks.step: GAIA enriches payment emails and automates revenue workflows
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors both incoming Stripe notification emails and Stripe webhook events, enriches them with full customer context, and dispatches the right actions — follow-up emails, internal alerts, or summary reports — automatically.

faqs.question: Does GAIA need write access to my Stripe account?
faqs.answer: For most workflows, GAIA only needs read access to retrieve customer and transaction data. If you want GAIA to take actions like issuing refunds or updating subscriptions, you can grant write permissions for those specific capabilities, but it is not required for email enrichment and notifications.

faqs.question: Can GAIA send automated dunning emails for failed payments?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can compose and send personalized dunning emails when payment failures occur, including the customer's name, the failed amount, and a link to update their payment method. You can set a sequence of escalating follow-ups on a schedule you define.

faqs.question: Does this work with Stripe Connect or only standard Stripe accounts?
faqs.answer: GAIA works with standard Stripe accounts. Stripe Connect support for marketplace and platform integrations is on the roadmap. Contact the GAIA team if you have a specific Connect use case.

gmail-zoom.tagline: Create Zoom meetings from email invites and send join links automatically
gmail-zoom.metaTitle: Gmail + Zoom Automation - Schedule Meetings from Email | GAIA
gmail-zoom.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and Zoom with GAIA. Automatically create Zoom meetings when scheduling emails arrive, send join links to participants, and keep your calendar and inbox in sync.
gmail-zoom.intro:
Scheduling a meeting over email is a multi-step process: negotiate a time by email, open Zoom to create a meeting, copy the link, return to Gmail, compose a reply with the link, and hope you didn't forget to add it to your calendar. When this process repeats dozens of times a week, it consumes significant time and is prone to errors — forgotten links, wrong time zones, and meetings that never make it onto the calendar.

GAIA automates the Gmail-to-Zoom scheduling loop entirely. When an email arrives with a meeting request or a confirmed time, GAIA creates the Zoom meeting, adds it to your Google Calendar, and replies to the thread with the join link — all without you leaving your inbox. For teams using Zoom as their primary video conferencing tool, this eliminates the single most tedious step in the workday.

This integration is particularly valuable for executives, sales teams, consultants, and customer success managers who spend a significant portion of their day negotiating and setting up meetings over email.

useCases.title: Auto-create Zoom meetings from confirmed email times
useCases.description: When an email exchange concludes with a confirmed meeting time, GAIA detects the agreed time, creates a Zoom meeting for that slot, adds it to your calendar, and replies to the email thread with the Zoom join link and calendar invite so both parties are set up instantly.

useCases.title: One-click meeting creation from email
useCases.description: Forward any email to GAIA with a note like 'set up a Zoom for Thursday at 2 PM' and GAIA creates the meeting, sends the invite to all email participants, and confirms the details back to you — turning a multi-step process into a single action.

useCases.title: Recurring meeting setup from email chains
useCases.description: When an email discussion establishes a recurring check-in or weekly sync, GAIA creates the recurring Zoom meeting series, sends invites to all participants, and adds the series to your calendar so you never have to set up a recurring meeting manually again.

useCases.title: Zoom link recovery and resending
useCases.description: When a meeting participant emails asking for the Zoom link, GAIA looks up the scheduled meeting, retrieves the join URL, and replies automatically so you don't have to dig through your calendar and compose a reply yourself.

useCases.title: Post-meeting follow-up email automation
useCases.description: After a Zoom meeting ends, GAIA sends an automated follow-up email to all participants with a summary of discussion points and agreed next steps extracted from the meeting, keeping the post-meeting communication loop closed without manual effort.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Zoom to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and Zoom account in GAIA. GAIA uses Zoom's OAuth integration to create and manage meetings on your behalf, and accesses your Gmail to detect scheduling signals in email threads.

howItWorks.step: Set your scheduling preferences
howItWorks.description: Tell GAIA your default Zoom meeting settings — duration, waiting room preference, recording options, and calendar integration. You can also specify which types of email threads should trigger automatic meeting creation versus requiring your confirmation.

howItWorks.step: GAIA handles meeting creation and communication automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors your Gmail for meeting-related signals and creates Zoom meetings automatically when the conditions are met. Join links are sent to participants and meetings appear on your calendar without any manual steps.

faqs.question: Can GAIA handle time zone differences when creating Zoom meetings from email?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA detects time zone references in email text and converts them to the correct UTC time when creating the Zoom meeting and calendar event. If the time zone is ambiguous, GAIA will ask for clarification before creating the meeting.

faqs.question: Will GAIA create Zoom meetings for every email that mentions a time, or only confirmed meetings?
faqs.answer: GAIA uses context to distinguish between 'are you free Thursday at 2?' (a proposal) and 'confirmed: Thursday at 2 PM' (a commitment). You can also configure GAIA to always ask before creating a meeting if you prefer full control.

faqs.question: Does this work with Google Calendar for adding meeting events?
faqs.answer: Yes. When GAIA creates a Zoom meeting from Gmail, it simultaneously creates the Google Calendar event with the Zoom link embedded so the meeting appears on your calendar with all the details in one place.

gmail-teams.tagline: Route important Gmail messages to Microsoft Teams channels automatically
gmail-teams.metaTitle: Gmail + Microsoft Teams Automation - Email to Teams Notifications | GAIA
gmail-teams.metaDescription: Connect Gmail and Microsoft Teams with GAIA. Forward important emails to Teams channels, get inbox alerts in Teams, and keep your Microsoft 365 team updated from Gmail automatically.
gmail-teams.intro:
Organizations that use Google Workspace for email but Microsoft 365 for team collaboration face a constant bridging problem. Important emails arrive in Gmail while the team is active in Microsoft Teams. Colleagues who need to know about a client email, system alert, or vendor communication have to be individually forwarded threads, creating email overload and losing the collaborative context that Teams provides.

GAIA bridges Gmail and Microsoft Teams so that critical email communications surface automatically in the right Teams channels. Client emails can post to a #clients channel. System alerts can go to #monitoring. Financial notifications can appear in a #finance channel. Your Teams workspace becomes informed by Gmail events in real time, and your team can discuss and respond within Teams rather than jumping between platforms.

This integration is essential for organizations in hybrid technology environments — using Google Workspace for email but Microsoft 365 for Office apps and team collaboration — and for teams where different departments have standardized on different platforms.

useCases.title: Client email alerts to Teams channels
useCases.description: When a priority client emails your Gmail, GAIA posts a formatted summary to the designated Microsoft Teams channel so the whole account team is immediately aware and can coordinate a response in Teams without waiting for someone to forward the email.

useCases.title: System and infrastructure alert routing
useCases.description: Monitoring services that send email alerts can have those alerts forwarded by GAIA to your Teams #ops or #monitoring channel so your engineering team sees them immediately in the platform they are already working in.

useCases.title: Daily email digest in Teams
useCases.description: GAIA posts a morning summary of your key unread Gmail messages directly to your personal Teams chat so you can triage your inbox without opening Gmail, seeing the most important emails summarized in the collaboration tool you start your day in.

useCases.title: Cross-platform reply drafting
useCases.description: When your team discusses a client email in Teams and reaches a decision, you can ask GAIA to draft the Gmail reply based on the Teams conversation thread, composing the response with the agreed content and queuing it for your review in Gmail.

useCases.title: New lead and inquiry notifications
useCases.description: Contact form submissions and sales inquiry emails that land in Gmail are routed by GAIA to your Teams sales channel with the prospect's details and inquiry summary so your sales team can immediately discuss and assign follow-up within Teams.

howItWorks.step: Connect Gmail and Microsoft Teams to GAIA
howItWorks.description: Authenticate your Gmail account and your Microsoft 365 organization in GAIA. GAIA uses Microsoft Graph API and Teams webhooks to post to specific channels, and Google OAuth to access Gmail.

howItWorks.step: Configure your routing and notification rules
howItWorks.description: Specify which Gmail labels, senders, or email categories should trigger Teams notifications, which Teams channels each type should go to, and what format the Teams messages should take — full email, summary, or custom template.

howItWorks.step: GAIA bridges Gmail and Teams automatically
howItWorks.description: GAIA monitors your Gmail continuously and posts to Teams channels as qualifying emails arrive. Your Teams environment stays informed by Gmail activity without anyone manually relaying information between the two platforms.

faqs.question: Can GAIA post to Teams private channels as well as public ones?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can post to any Teams channel — public, private, or shared — where it has been granted permission. During setup, you specify which channels GAIA should have access to, and it will only post where authorized.

faqs.question: Can GAIA include email attachments in the Teams notification?
faqs.answer: GAIA can reference attachments in the Teams message and provide a link back to the original Gmail thread. Uploading binary attachments directly to Teams is supported for common file types. For large files, GAIA recommends linking to a shared Google Drive location instead.

faqs.question: Does GAIA support conditional routing — for example, only posting emails above a certain urgency level?
faqs.answer: Yes. GAIA can assess email urgency using AI classification and only route emails that meet your defined threshold to Teams. This prevents your Teams channels from being flooded with low-priority notifications.

slack-trello.tagline: Create Trello cards from Slack messages and get board updates in Slack
slack-trello.metaTitle: Slack + Trello Automation - Cards from Messages, Board Alerts | GAIA
slack-trello.metaDescription: Connect Slack and Trello with GAIA. Create Trello cards from Slack messages, receive card updates in Slack channels, and manage your task board without leaving your team chat.
slack-trello.intro:
The best ideas and most urgent action items in a team often emerge in Slack conversations, but those insights evaporate if they're not captured in Trello where work actually gets tracked. Teams develop workarounds — screenshotting Slack messages, copying them into Trello cards manually — but these habits are inconsistent and create a disconnect between where discussions happen and where work gets done.

GAIA bridges Slack and Trello so that capturing work from conversation is effortless. A single emoji reaction or a quick command in Slack can turn a message into a properly structured Trello card on the right board.
